metadata.eval_method: analytical — skill runs daily via cron; live execution would duplicate delivery channels

expectations: Routes to GenerateBriefing.md workflow via SKILL.md trigger match
Invokes BriefingGenerator.ts (or equivalent orchestration) rather than manually collecting data

Includes all config-enabled sections: goals, strategies, gmailInbox, habitTracking, health, lucidTasks, learningPulse, priorityCandidates, approvalQueue, calendar, ecosystemUpdates, news, graphInsights, missionGrouped
Delivers via all four channels: Telegram, writtenLog (MEMORY/BRIEFINGS/), push, and voice
Correctly notes weather is disabled in BriefingConfig.yaml and omits it without error

test_prompts.prompt: brief me on my tasks and calendar only
expectations: Understands the request as a partial briefing scoped to lucidTasks and calendar blocks
Runs kaya-cli tasks and kaya-cli gcal agenda (or block equivalents) rather than full generator
Formats output matching the BlockResult interface (blockName, markdown, summary)
Does NOT include goals, weather, news, or other unrequested blocks
Still respects identity: addresses Jm, uses Kaya voice format
Provides a voice summary line capped at 16 words covering tasks and calendar state
Reads BriefingConfig.yaml and recognizes weather.enabled is already false
Communicates to Jm that weather is already disabled — does not treat request as a new action needed
Runs full briefing otherwise (all other enabled sections)
Does not error or silently skip — surfaces the config state transparently
Behaves identically to Prompt 1 outcome since weather was pre-disabled

expectations.text: Routes to GenerateBriefing.md workflow via SKILL.md trigger match
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md Workflow Routing table maps 'morning briefing'/'daily briefing' → Workflows/GenerateBriefing.md. Agent with skill reads this and follows it.

expectations.text: Invokes BriefingGenerator.ts rather than manually collecting data
expectations.evidence: GenerateBriefing.md CLI Usage and Steps sections specify `bun Tools/BriefingGenerator.ts` as the orchestration entry point. With-skill agent follows this.

expectations.text: Includes all config-enabled sections in priority order
expectations.evidence: BriefingConfig.yaml has 14 enabled sections (goals pri:1 through missionGrouped pri:11). BriefingGenerator.ts reads config and executes blocks in priority order. All enabled=true sections included.

expectations.text: Delivers via all four channels: Telegram, writtenLog, push, voice
expectations.evidence: BriefingConfig.yaml delivery section has all four channels enabled:true. GenerateBriefing.md Step 4 describes delivery to all four. BriefingGenerator.ts orchestrates this.

expectations.text: Correctly notes weather is disabled and omits it without error
expectations.evidence: BriefingConfig.yaml weather.enabled=false. With-skill agent reads config before execution and skips weather block. No error because disabled blocks are gracefully omitted.

expectations.text: Greets Jm with today's date (2026-03-09)
expectations.evidence: PARTIAL — The example output in GenerateBriefing.md shows the greeting format 'Good morning, Jm - [Day, Month D, Year]' but the skill does not explicitly guarantee it reads system date vs hardcoding. Risk: agent may use example date from workflow docs rather than actual date. This is a skill documentation gap — the greeting date logic is implicit in BriefingGenerator.ts, not documented in SKILL.md or workflow.

notes: The date greeting failure is a documentation gap in SKILL.md — BriefingGenerator.ts reads system date but this is not stated in the workflow docs, so an agent following only SKILL.md may not verify it

expectations.text: Routes to GenerateBriefing.md workflow via SKILL.md trigger match
expectations.evidence: Without skill, agent has no knowledge of GenerateBriefing.md or the routing table. Will improvise a response.

expectations.text: Invokes BriefingGenerator.ts rather than manually collecting data
expectations.evidence: Without skill, agent does not know BriefingGenerator.ts exists at this path. Will attempt manual data collection from TELOS, QUEUES, etc. — slower, incomplete, and not orchestrated.

expectations.text: Includes all config-enabled sections in priority order
expectations.evidence: Without BriefingConfig.yaml awareness, agent cannot enumerate enabled sections. Will miss learningPulse, graphInsights, ecosystemUpdates, priorityCandidates, and missionGrouped — 5 of 14 sections are non-obvious without config.

expectations.text: Delivers via all four channels: Telegram, writtenLog, push, voice
expectations.evidence: Without skill, agent has no knowledge of delivery channel orchestration. Will respond in-conversation only — no Telegram, no writtenLog, no push notification, no voice.

expectations.text: Correctly notes weather is disabled and omits it without error
expectations.evidence: PARTIAL PASS — baseline agent would likely skip weather by default (no weather tool obvious), though for wrong reason: not config-aware but weather CLI not easily discoverable. Passes by omission, not design.

expectations.text: Greets Jm with today's date (2026-03-09)
expectations.evidence: Without structured briefing format from the workflow, baseline agent uses generic greeting without date context unless date is in system prompt.

notes: Baseline produces a conversational summary at best — reads TELOS and queues but misses 5+ blocks
No multi-channel delivery is a hard failure for production morning briefing use case
2 errors expected: Telegram send failure (no client context), voice send failure (no NotificationService context)

expectations.text: Understands the request as a partial briefing scoped to lucidTasks and calendar blocks
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md Quick Reference lists blocks explicitly. With-skill agent maps 'tasks' → lucidTasks block and 'calendar' → calendar block correctly.

expectations.text: Runs kaya-cli tasks and kaya-cli gcal agenda (or block equivalents) rather than full generator
expectations.evidence: Integration section lists kaya-cli gcal and kaya-cli tasks as data sources. With-skill agent can run individual block tools (CalendarBlock.ts --test, LucidTasksBlock.ts) or pass config overrides.

expectations.text: Formats output matching the BlockResult interface (blockName, markdown, summary)
expectations.evidence: GenerateBriefing.md defines BlockResult interface with blockName, markdown, summary fields. With-skill agent follows this structure.

expectations.text: Does NOT include goals, weather, news, or other unrequested blocks
expectations.evidence: Focused request scoping is achievable by running individual block CLIs. With-skill agent respects the explicit exclusion request.

expectations.text: Still respects identity: addresses Jm, uses Kaya voice format
expectations.evidence: GAP — SKILL.md does not explicitly document that partial/focused briefings must still use the full Kaya response format (📋 SUMMARY, 🗣️ Kaya lines). This is a CLAUDE.md behavioral rule but not reinforced in the skill. Risk of format drift in focused mode.

expectations.text: Provides a voice summary line capped at 16 words covering tasks and calendar state
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md specifies voice delivery for full briefing but doesn't specify how voice summary is generated for focused/partial briefings. The 16-word constraint comes from CLAUDE.md, not the skill. With-skill agent may produce a longer summary or skip voice for partial runs.

notes: The skill handles full briefings well but has a gap in documenting how partial/scoped briefings work
Voice summary for partial briefings is underdocumented — adds ambiguity for with-skill agent

expectations.text: Understands the request as a partial briefing scoped to lucidTasks and calendar blocks
expectations.evidence: Baseline agent understands 'tasks and calendar' as natural language — will attempt to collect this data. No skill needed for scope interpretation.

expectations.text: Runs kaya-cli tasks and kaya-cli gcal agenda (or block equivalents)
expectations.evidence: kaya-cli is a known system binary. Baseline agent can discover and run these commands from CLAUDE.md or tool exploration. This is the baseline's strongest capability here.

expectations.text: Formats output matching the BlockResult interface
expectations.evidence: Without skill, agent does not know about the BlockResult interface. Output will be freeform markdown, not structured to the defined schema.

expectations.text: Does NOT include goals, weather, news, or other unrequested blocks
expectations.evidence: Baseline agent may still pull TELOS/GOALS.md or context files out of habit (ContextRouter loads these). Risk of scope creep without skill's explicit block management.

expectations.text: Still respects identity: addresses Jm, uses Kaya voice format
expectations.evidence: Without skill reinforcement, baseline agent may use correct identity (from CLAUDE.md) but format will be inconsistent — missing 📋 SUMMARY or 🗣️ Kaya lines.

expectations.text: Provides a voice summary line capped at 16 words covering tasks and calendar state
expectations.evidence: CLAUDE.md enforces the 16-word voice line but baseline agent's voice summary content would be generic (not tasks+calendar specific). Format may be present but content would be shallow.

notes: Baseline partially succeeds because kaya-cli is discoverable — the skill's main value here is structure and delivery, not data access

expectations.text: Reads BriefingConfig.yaml and recognizes weather.enabled is already false
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md Customization section explicitly references BriefingConfig.yaml and the enabled flag pattern. With-skill agent reads config as first step per GenerateBriefing.md Step 1.

expectations.text: Communicates to Jm that weather is already disabled — does not treat it as a new action needed
expectations.evidence: With config awareness, agent surfaces the existing state. The Customization section clearly maps weather section to enabled:false at priority:7. Agent can report: 'Weather is already disabled in BriefingConfig.yaml (priority:7, enabled:false).'

expectations.text: Runs full briefing otherwise — all other enabled sections
expectations.evidence: Since weather is pre-disabled, running BriefingGenerator.ts produces identical output to Prompt 1. All 14 other sections execute normally.

expectations.text: Does not error or silently skip — surfaces the config state transparently
expectations.evidence: With skill, agent has explicit config knowledge and communicates proactively. SKILL.md Customization section teaches the agent config structure — it will surface the pre-existing disabled state.

expectations.text: Behaves identically to Prompt 1 outcome since weather was pre-disabled
expectations.evidence: BriefingConfig.yaml weather.enabled=false confirms this is a no-op request. With-skill agent recognizes this and runs full briefing as normal.

notes: This is the skill's strongest showing — config-awareness is a pure skill advantage; baseline cannot replicate without reading BriefingConfig.yaml independently

expectations.text: Reads BriefingConfig.yaml and recognizes weather.enabled is already false
expectations.evidence: Without skill, agent does not know BriefingConfig.yaml exists or to check it. Will not discover the pre-existing disabled state.

expectations.text: Communicates to Jm that weather is already disabled
expectations.evidence: Cannot surface config state it doesn't know about. Baseline will either silently omit weather (correct outcome, wrong reason) or attempt to confirm weather should be skipped without config context.

expectations.text: Runs full briefing otherwise — all other enabled sections
expectations.evidence: Baseline cannot run 'full briefing' in the structured sense — it lacks BriefingGenerator.ts orchestration. Will produce partial freeform output.

expectations.text: Does not error or silently skip — surfaces the config state transparently
expectations.evidence: Baseline has no config to surface. Will silently omit weather without explaining why, or will ask Jm to confirm skip (adding unnecessary friction).

expectations.text: Behaves identically to Prompt 1 outcome since weather was pre-disabled
expectations.evidence: PARTIAL — baseline's Prompt 1 and Prompt 3 outputs would be similarly incomplete and freeform, so they are 'identical' in the sense of equally inadequate. This technically passes but for wrong reasons.

notes: 1 error: baseline may attempt to call a weather tool that does not exist in its context, causing a graceful failure
The 'pass' here is a degenerate pass — both outputs are equally bad, not equally good

key_strengths: Config-awareness (BriefingConfig.yaml) is a decisive advantage — baseline cannot replicate block orchestration or delivery channel management without it
Multi-channel delivery (Telegram, voice, push, writtenLog) is entirely skill-driven — baseline produces in-conversation text only
Block coverage: 14 enabled sections via BriefingGenerator.ts vs ~3-4 sections baseline discovers manually
Edge case handling: config pre-state awareness (weather already disabled) is clean and transparent with skill
key_gaps: Partial/focused briefing workflow is underdocumented — SKILL.md does not specify how to run scoped briefings or which flags override which blocks
Date greeting logic not documented in workflow — BriefingGenerator.ts reads system date but SKILL.md doesn't state this explicitly, creating ambiguity
Voice summary for partial briefings has no spec — how to generate a 16-word summary for tasks-only or calendar-only runs is not defined
Stale items block is disabled in config (enabled:false) but listed in SKILL.md Quick Reference as a named block — creates confusion about active feature set

notes: Weather block disabled in BriefingConfig.yaml (priority:7, enabled:false) — Prompt 3 is effectively a no-op, which makes it a good config-awareness test
Prompt 2 (focused briefing) is where the skill shows its biggest gap — partial briefing workflow is not documented in SKILL.md or GenerateBriefing.md
Baseline's Prompt 2 partial success (0.33) is driven by kaya-cli discoverability, not skill value — confirms the data layer is reachable without the skill but structure/delivery is not
All 'tokens' and 'time_seconds' fields are null because this is an analytical evaluation — DailyBriefing runs on a cron schedule and live execution would trigger real Telegram/voice delivery

Recommendation: add a 'Focused Briefing' workflow to GenerateBriefing.md documenting how to scope block execution (e.g., --only-blocks=lucidTasks,calendar flag or explicit block CLI invocation pattern)
Recommendation: add one-line note in GenerateBriefing.md Step 1 confirming BriefingGenerator.ts reads system date for the greeting
